THE TORNADO STARTED NEAR MARION AND RAUCHOLZ ROADS WITH TREE LIMB DAMAGE. THE TORNADO INTENSIFIED TO EF-1 ALONG FORDNEY ROAD BETWEEN BRANT AND FERGUS ROADS. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED AND MANY MORE WERE SEVERELY DAMAGED. MINOR TO MODERATE STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED WITH ROOF DAMAGE AND DESTRUCTION OF OUT-BUILDINGS...INCLUDING A COLLAPSED GARAGE. THE TORNADO ENDED 1 MILE SOUTH OF ST CHARLES WHERE ADDITIONAL EF-0 DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED.

WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 75 MPH. FIRE OFFICIALS REPORTED SEVERAL TRAILERS BEING BLOWN OVER AS WELL AS A ROOF TO A GARAGE BEING TORN OFF.

THE TORNADO STARTED NEAR LAMBETH PARK AND NEW KENT ROAD AND TRACKED EAST-NORTHEAST...ENDING NEAR WEST RIDGE DRIVE AND SPRING CREEK ROAD. THE TORNADO IS RATED EF-1 WITH MAXIMUM WIND GUSTS OF 90 MPH. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 150 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH WAS 1 1/4 MILES. STRONGEST DAMAGE WAS BETWEEN GRANDVIEW AND FAIRLANE DRIVES WITH PARTIAL ROOF TORN OFF OF HOUSE AND SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED ALONG THE PATH. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 559 AM.
SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE CENTERED NEAR GRANDVIEW AND FAIRVIEW IN ROCHESTER HILLS EXTENDING FOR ABOUT SIX BLOCKS. TREES DOWN AND ROOFS OFF SOME HOUSES.
